videos.title: 07.07.26 - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
07.07.26 - RIFT Presentation RIFT is a modern trading platform that combines automated trading, manual trading, futures trading, and educational tools into one ecosystem.

Designed for both beginners and experienced traders, RIFT aims to simplify access to financial markets while providing users with tools that can help them participate more efficiently.

One of the key features of RIFT is its Auto Trading Engine, which allows users to allocate capital into automated trading strategies designed to operate without the need for constant monitoring. This enables participants to remain active in the markets without spending hours each day analyzing charts or placing trades manually.

For users who prefer direct market participation, RIFT also offers Manual Trading and Futures Trading functionality. This gives traders the flexibility to execute their own strategies, manage positions, and take advantage of market opportunities in real time.

RIFT is built around a non-custodial philosophy, meaning users maintain control of their own funds rather than depositing them into a centralized company account. This approach aligns with the growing demand for greater transparency, security, and user control within the digital asset industry.

Beyond trading, RIFT focuses heavily on education. Through training sessions, community support, and ongoing learning resources, users can develop a deeper understanding of trading, risk management, market psychology, and financial technology.

The platform also incorporates modern technologies such as AI-driven infrastructure, advanced execution systems, copy trading capabilities, and future integrations designed to create a more connected and intelligent trading environment.

Whether you are completely new to trading, looking for automated solutions, interested in futures markets, or seeking to expand your financial knowledge, RIFT aims to provide a complete ecosystem where users can learn, participate, and grow.

Key Features

• Auto Trading Engine
• Manual Trading
• Futures Trading
• Non-Custodial Infrastructure
• AI-Powered Trading Tools
• Copy Trading Features
• Educational Resources & Training
• Community Support
• Advanced Market Access
• Designed for Beginners and Experienced Traders

RIFT is built around a simple vision: making modern trading technology more accessible while helping users participate in financial markets through a combination of automation, education, and innovative infrastructure. videos.title: The two-quarterback debate + 5 other factors that will impact CSU’s ceiling in 2026
videos.summary_source_text:
The two-quarterback debate + 5 other factors that will impact CSU’s ceiling in 2026 Justin Michael opens up with some thoughts on the USMNT coming up short, plus a brief update on how Nique Clifford and Isaiah Stevens are doing in NBA Summer League and Trey McBride’s controversial interview. Justin then talks about QB Anthoney Hill in Remember that Ram before moving the discussion to Hauss Hejny and K’Saan Farrar. Justin talks about the pros and cons of not having an established starter, plus why he’s genuinely excited about both guys. Finally, Justin gives out five other key factors that will impact the success of CSU’s QB’s in 2026.

videos.title: Should the Rockies hold or trade Jake McCarthy?
videos.summary_source_text:
Should the Rockies hold or trade Jake McCarthy? While 2026 has been much improved for the Rockies compared to 2025, the team is very much still considered sellers at the deadline. That being said, a lot of people have mixed beliefs about what the Rockies should do with outfielder Jake McCarthy. Should the Rockies keep him as part of their future or trade him to free up space in the outfield? Christian Saez and Spencer Smith discuss on the DNVR Rockies podcast.

videos.title: Hindi - 07.07.26 - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
Hindi - 07.07.26 - RIFT Presentation RIFT is a modern trading platform that combines automated trading, manual trading, futures trading, and educational tools into one ecosystem.

Designed for both beginners and experienced traders, RIFT aims to simplify access to financial markets while providing users with tools that can help them participate more efficiently.

One of the key features of RIFT is its Auto Trading Engine, which allows users to allocate capital into automated trading strategies designed to operate without the need for constant monitoring. This enables participants to remain active in the markets without spending hours each day analyzing charts or placing trades manually.

For users who prefer direct market participation, RIFT also offers Manual Trading and Futures Trading functionality. This gives traders the flexibility to execute their own strategies, manage positions, and take advantage of market opportunities in real time.

RIFT is built around a non-custodial philosophy, meaning users maintain control of their own funds rather than depositing them into a centralized company account. This approach aligns with the growing demand for greater transparency, security, and user control within the digital asset industry.

Beyond trading, RIFT focuses heavily on education. Through training sessions, community support, and ongoing learning resources, users can develop a deeper understanding of trading, risk management, market psychology, and financial technology.

The platform also incorporates modern technologies such as AI-driven infrastructure, advanced execution systems, copy trading capabilities, and future integrations designed to create a more connected and intelligent trading environment.

Whether you are completely new to trading, looking for automated solutions, interested in futures markets, or seeking to expand your financial knowledge, RIFT aims to provide a complete ecosystem where users can learn, participate, and grow.

Key Features

• Auto Trading Engine
• Manual Trading
• Futures Trading
• Non-Custodial Infrastructure
• AI-Powered Trading Tools
• Copy Trading Features
• Educational Resources & Training
• Community Support
• Advanced Market Access
• Designed for Beginners and Experienced Traders

videos.title: The Avalanche Explored a Hellebuyck Trade. What Young Guns Could Step Up This Season?
videos.summary_source_text:
The Avalanche Explored a Hellebuyck Trade. What Young Guns Could Step Up This Season? Colorado Avalanche considered a blockbuster move for Connor Hellebuyck, eyeing a league-altering goalie upgrade that could have reshaped the Stanley Cup race. Could bringing the “world’s best goalie” to Denver have changed the Avalanche’s trajectory? Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson break down the rumors from Elliotte Friedman’s 32 Thoughts, exploring trade logistics, potential assets on the move, and how Hellebuyck in net might have meant no regression for Colorado.

The hosts debate Mackenzie Blackwood’s place, Scott Wedgwood’s breakout season, and the Avalanche’s commitment to a modern goaltending tandem versus superstar contracts. Discussion shifts to Colorado’s youth movement: Can players like Gavin Brinley and Taylor Makar seize NHL roles in a “win-now” environment? Insightful takes on prospect opportunities, roster construction, and playoff implications make this a can’t-miss listen for fans tracking the Avalanche’s quest for hockey supremacy.

James Carey Lauder-Imagn Images

videos.title: Wyndham Clark Hate Needs to Stop + The U.S. Was the Perfect FIFA World Cup Host | Closed Course
videos.summary_source_text:
Wyndham Clark Hate Needs to Stop + The U.S. Was the Perfect FIFA World Cup Host | Closed Course Welcome back to another episode of Closed Course!

The gang is back together for another episode talking golf, the FIFA World Cup, and big plans for the summer.

The excitement surrounding the FIFA World Cup continues to grow as the knockout rounds progress. The guys discuss Team USA's impressive run and their chances of hanging on for the remainder of the tournament. They also talk about the success of the United States hosting the FIFA World Cup and how people from around the world have responded to their time in the U.S. Regardless of who advances, the tournament is set to deliver entertaining football all the way to the final.

The guys also discuss recent professional golf tournaments, specifically the U.S. Open at Shinnecock Hills Golf Club, where Colorado native Wyndham Clark captured his second U.S. Open Championship. Despite a very successful tournament for Clark, it didn't come easy, as he received heavy criticism and scrutiny for his past actions. The hosts discuss the unnecessary hate Clark receives and why golf fans should move on from mistakes he's made in the past.

From golf talk and World Cup banter to must-hear stories from Blake's wedding in Croatia, this is a must-listen episode of the Closed Course Podcast.

Tune in every Tuesday for new episodes of Closed Course.

Powered by Pins & Aces

videos.summary_source_text:
AI Can FINALLY Edit Videos For You Imagine typing "edit this for me" and letting AI handle your entire video timeline. 🤯

That's the promise behind Palmier, a new AI video editor powered by Claude. From organizing media and trimming clips to generating missing B-roll on the fly, it’s designed to automate the manual side of editing.

At $29/month for around 3 to 7 minutes of generated video, it's not the cheapest product to use, especially when you factor in potential revisions. BUT it's still probably cheaper than a professional video editor. 

I haven’t run any footage through it yet, but I'm curious to see how it performs and if it's worth it. Let me know in the comments if you'd want to see a hands-on test 👇🏻

videos.title: OFFER SHEET CHAOS! Carolina Hurricanes Rumored to be Interested in Simon Edvinsson
videos.summary_source_text:
OFFER SHEET CHAOS! Carolina Hurricanes Rumored to be Interested in Simon Edvinsson During the mega final episode of 32 Thoughts before the summer break, Elliotte Freidman stated that the Carolina Hurricanes were looking to offer sheet a couple of players. One of them as Leo Carlsson, but the one that caught everyone's attention was Detroit Red Wings' Simon Edvinsson. That being said, does it make sense for the Hurricanes to offer sheet for Edvinsson, and would the Red Wings match it? On the Wednesday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on the news of the Hurricanes being interested in Edvinsson. While they are still figure out what to do with Alexander Nikishin, does going after Edvinsson makes sense if nothing works out? Secondly, if the Hurricanes do off sheet hime, how would that work overall with cap space and pick compensation to the Wings? Plus, how would this change the dynamics of the Hurricanes moving forward if this does play out? Finally, Zach concludes the show by shares his thoughts on where the team is at currently. While they have not made many moves, there is a sense that this will be more quieter than usual when it comes to roster constructon that summers past. All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: LeBron to Denver? Sean Payton's Belichick Plan & USMNT World Cup Collapse | After the Horn
videos.summary_source_text:
LeBron to Denver? Sean Payton's Belichick Plan & USMNT World Cup Collapse | After the Horn Ron Zappolo, Woody Paige, and Tom Green are back for another episode of After the Horn, diving into the biggest stories in Denver sports and beyond.

🏀 Could LeBron James actually become a Denver Nugget? The guys debate Denver's chances of landing the NBA superstar and what a partnership with Nikola Jokic would look like.

🏈 The crew reacts to the stunning report that Sean Payton once floated the idea of stepping aside so Bill Belichick could coach the Broncos long enough to break Don Shula's all-time wins record.

⚽ Plus, thoughts on Team USA's disappointing World Cup exit against Belgium and what comes next for American soccer on the world stage.

⚾ The Rockies continue to battle through another difficult season, but are things finally trending in the right direction in Colorado?

As always, expect plenty of stories, laughs, disagreements, and classic banter from Ron, Woody, and Tom.

videos.title: The MacKinnon Evasion Pattern
videos.summary_source_text:
The MacKinnon Evasion Pattern The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

From Julian Lewis’ growth under Brennan Marion to Danny Scudero’s breakout potential, Ben Finneseth’s leadership, Naeten Mitchell’s edge, and a Buffs roster embracing the underdog role, Colorado football feels like it has a new urgency heading into the Big 12 season. I go step by step through ChatGPT memory, projects, project sources, meeting recordings, tasks, spreadsheets, dashboards, image editing, and agent mode. I also show how ChatGPT can clean a messy spreadsheet and turn it into an interactive dashboard with filters.

Julian Lewis, Naeten Mitchell, Ben Finneseth and Fernando Lovo join us from Big 12 Media Day On today’s DNVR Buffs Podcast, Jake Schwanitz and Scott Procter are joined by Colorado Football players Julian Lewis, Naeten Mitchell, Ben Finneseth and new Colorado Athletic Director Fernando Lovo from Big 12 Media Days in Frisco, TX! Why Coach Prime is “pissed off” heading into the 2026 season, his expectations for Julian Lewis in the Go-Go Offense and an update on his health heading into his fourth season as Colorado’s head coach. videos.title: What Is Your Food Telling Your Body?
videos.summary_source_text:
What Is Your Food Telling Your Body? Every time you eat, your body is listening. Food doesn't just provide fuel, it sends signals that influence hormones, metabolism, and the way your body functions. That's why nutrition is about more than counting calories. It's about understanding the message you're sending with every meal.

To learn more about how to book a consultation with me please check out https://aristotleeducation.com

All of my social media and official links: https://linktr.ee/pradipjamnadasmd

Subscribe to the Channel! https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCOtQHehGWtblMp1gZC8Kq3Q?sub_confirmation=1

Remember that my videos are not a substitute for individualized medical care by a qualified physician. Always consult your physicians before making any diet, nutrition, medication, or lifestyle changes.


About Dr. Pradip Jamnadas, MD, MBBS, FACC, FSCAI, FCCP, FACP

The founder and Chief Medical Officer of Aristotle Education and has been practicing medicine in Central Florida for over 35 years. He is also the founder and medical director of Cardiovascular Interventions, PA, where since 1990 he has been repeatedly recognized in local publications as a Top Doctor. Throughout his distinguished career, he has performed thousands of interventional procedures in both hospital and outpatient settings. As a consultant cardiologist with a large diversified inpatient and outpatient practice he is noted for his passions for teaching and illuminating prevention for cardiovascular disease. He is also a clinical assistant professor of medicine at The Florida State University and University of Central Florida. Every time you eat, your body is listening. ESPN’s Seth Wickersham on HOW CLOSE Belichick was to Becoming the Broncos HC with Sean Payton & More ESPN senior writer Seth Wickersham joins DNVR Broncos to break down his inside access to Sean Payton, including how close Bill Belichick was to becoming the Denver Broncos head coach and what that wild partnership could have looked like. Wickersham shares behind-the-scenes details on Payton’s playoff preparation, his relationship with Bo Nix, the Davis Webb play-calling transition, and why Payton’s creative approach keeps shaping the Broncos’ future. Plus, the crew dives into the fake punt vs. Josh Allen and the Buffalo Bills, the toll on Denver’s coaching staff, and what it all means for the Broncos as they chase a Super Bowl.

videos.title: THE #1 WAY TO HELP FADE DARK SPOTS | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
THE #1 WAY TO HELP FADE DARK SPOTS | Dr. Mandell Dark spots, age spots, acne marks, and uneven skin tone can be frustrating, but one of the most effective skincare ingredients is also one of the simplest and most affordable. In this video, Dr. Alan Mandell explains the science behind vitamin C serum and how it may help brighten your complexion, reduce the appearance of hyperpigmentation, support collagen production, and protect your skin from everyday environmental damage. You'll learn why dark spots develop, how melanin is produced, the important role of the enzyme tyrosinase, and why vitamin C has become one of the most recommended ingredients by skincare professionals.

I also explain exactly what to look for when purchasing a vitamin C serum. Choose a product containing 10% to 20% vitamin C, preferably L-ascorbic acid, which is considered the most researched and effective form of vitamin C for the skin. Look for a serum packaged in a dark amber, opaque, or air-tight bottle, since light and oxygen can break down vitamin C and reduce its effectiveness. If the serum changes from clear or light yellow to dark orange or brown, it has oxidized and should be replaced. Remember, the most expensive product isn't always the best—many excellent vitamin C serums are affordable and available at Walmart, Target, CVS, Walgreens, Costco, Ulta Beauty, Sephora, Amazon, and most pharmacies.

You'll also learn exactly how to apply vitamin C serum, when to use it, how often to use it, why sunscreen is essential to prevent new pigmentation from forming, and how consistency is the key to achieving brighter, smoother, healthier-looking skin. Most people who respond begin noticing improvements within four to eight weeks, while deeper pigmentation may take longer. Healthy skin is not about finding one miracle product—it's about understanding the science, protecting your skin every day, and following a routine that works. If you notice rapidly changing skin lesions, irregular pigmentation, bleeding spots, or persistent discoloration, be sure to consult your dermatologist for a proper evaluation. If you found this video helpful, please subscribe, like, and share it with someone who wants healthier, younger-looking skin naturally.

*********************************

The Dallas Mavericks have reportedly agreed to a contract with Fenerbahce sniper Tarik Biberović, according to our own Marc Stein and other reports out of Europe. What will he bring to the team? And when will the Marcus Sasser deal get done? And what might the Mavs do with the rest of their mid-level exception? The DLLS Mavs crew explores those questions and much more, plus previews the team’s run in the Las Vegas Summer League, live on YouTube!

videos.title: Carlsson Offer Sheet Fallout and the NHL Player Empowerment Era ft. Tyler Johnson & Greg Wyshynski
videos.summary_source_text:
Carlsson Offer Sheet Fallout and the NHL Player Empowerment Era ft. Tyler Johnson & Greg Wyshynski On today’s episode of The Sheet, Jeff Marek is joined by Greg Wyshynski for another edition of MVSW (Marek vs. Wyshynski) as they break down the biggest stories shaking up the NHL offseason. Jeff and Greg dive into Leo Carlsson’s massive offer sheet with the Philadelphia Flyers, the ticking seven-day window for the Anaheim Ducks to make their decision, and how Pavel Mintyukov’s new contract impacts Anaheim’s salary cap situation moving forward. They discuss whether offer sheets are becoming a bigger weapon for NHL general managers, how teams are exploiting market inefficiencies, and which restricted free agents could become the next targets.

Then, two-time Stanley Cup champion Tyler Johnson joins the show to give a player’s perspective on the changing era of player empowerment in the NHL. Johnson discusses how players inside the Flyers and Ducks locker rooms might view Danny Briere’s aggressive offer sheet strategy, Leo Carlsson’s decision to sign, and what these moves mean from a player perspective. Plus, the guys dive into the latest around Brady Tkachuk, player podcasts, trade requests, Dylan Larkin’s reported preferred destinations, Zach Werenski rumours, no-trade clauses, and how much power NHL stars have in today’s league.

Jeff Marek, Greg Wyshynski, and Tyler Johnson cover all that and more on another loaded offseason edition of The Sheet.

#TheSheet #NHL #Hockey #Flyers #FlyTogether #FlyersHockey #AnaheimDucks #LetsGoDucks #LeoCarlsson #NHLOffseason #NHLFreeAgency #StanleyCup

Agent OS Q&A: Shopify→Amazon Automation, Fable 5 Alternatives, OmniRoot & Hermes Workflows

The episode answers community questions about an agent operating system that centralizes AI agents and custom workflows, highlighting new integrations like Tencent’s open-source Hunyuan 3 (HY3) and OmniRoot, a free open-source coding tool routing across ~90+ model providers. It explains how to automate Shopify listing edits and Amazon publishing by giving agents the Shopify API/CLI and Amazon seller API, and suggests converting Shopify to WordPress by exporting via Shopify CLI and rewriting with Claude. The host discusses Fable 5’s imminent subscription removal, recommending built-in alternatives (Hermes Mixture of Agents, Fusion, and Sakana Fugu) and noting special prompts aren’t necessary. Other topics include Handy as a WhisperFlow-style dictation tool, Open Carousel for carousel creation, using GLM 5.2 over HY3 for coding, client acquisition via outbound activity formulas, Hermes Astros for automated competitor keyword monitoring and content ideas, and VPS sandbox setups; it closes by inviting viewers to join the AI Profit Ballroom community for daily updates, guides, coaching calls, and Agent OS resources.

In this build I wire xAI's brand new Voice Agent Builder into Twilio, so an AI answers, screens the caller, and lines them up to go on air. Name, location and question straight onto a lower third. I'll show you what the launch handles for you in about two minutes, what's still on you to build, and the gotchas nobody mentions... consent, mix minus, and the one setting that stops trolls torching your bill. videos.summary_source_text:
Koenigsegg Gemera: 2 300 hk, V8, plats för fyra! Plus Legobilen! Den första kundbilen av Koenigsegg Gemera är äntligen färdig för leverans till den första kunden – och ställdes ut på The Aurora 2026.
Ytterligare 299 väntar på sin bil.
Och vilken bil! Totalt 2 300 hk med en V8 på 1 500 hk och tre elmotorer, torque vectoring på alla hjul, bakhjulsstyrning, dubbla mugghållare för alla och plats för fyra kabinväskor.
0–100 km/h på 1,9 sekunder.
Jag tar också en titt på världens snabbaste Legobil, en Koenigsegg Sadairs Spear – som man kan köpa stt eget exemplar av – måvara lite mindre och byggd av färre delar.

videos.title: Upper Body Power Drill – Ball First Board
videos.summary_source_text:
Upper Body Power Drill – Ball First Board Click link in bio for lowest price on the Ball First Board. Most golfers only throw their hips through the downswing and leave a ton of power on the table — real speed comes from your upper body joining in too.

This video uses a simple kettlebell throw analogy to show how your whole body, not just your hips, creates the momentum you need to really rip it.

You'll learn how to let your upper body follow your hips at the top of the swing so all that momentum transfers through impact, giving you more speed and distance without changing your swing mechanics.

We used the Ball First Board to help feel and confirm this correct sequencing at the top of the swing.

Like this video if you want more distance, and check the link in my bio for the Ball First Board.

• A pinch of cinnamon

Apple cider vinegar helps stabilize blood sugar, reduce appetite, and slow the breakdown of carbohydrates into glucose. Cinnamon helps support insulin sensitivity and blood sugar regulation.

For even better results, pair it with a walk after your meal. Better blood sugar control may also help with:
• Brain fog

• Cravings
• Hunger

How to "Vibe Direct" Short Films We've gone from vibe coding to vibe DIRECTING.

Instead of prompting individual clips and stitching everything together afterward, you can now direct an entire video through conversation.

I used @openart_ai Director to create my own horror movie trailer by describing the story, characters, pacing, visuals, and voice performances, then refining everything through chat.

It can generate up to 5 minutes (NOT seconds!) of continuous video while keeping the characters, voices, and story consistent the whole way through.

If you’re interested in trying your hand at vibe directing, head to OpenArt.ai to get started.

Inside My Claude Agent Operating System (Agent OS): Voice Agents, Content Factory, Memory & Token Diet

videos.title: Peugeot 408 Review: DON’T buy an SUV before watching this!
videos.summary_source_text:
Peugeot 408 Review: DON’T buy an SUV before watching this! This is our in-depth review of the updated Peugeot 408, reviewing the exterior design changes and additions to the interior, while also discussing how much it costs and what it’s like to drive. 

Get 20% off Car Vertical using code Carwow: https://www.carvertical.com/carwow

This is the new Peugeot 408! 

The 408 has undergone a mid-life facelift, so rather than undergoing a full overhaul, a couple of features and elements have been updated. 

The question is, are the updates enough to steer you away from key competitors like the Cupra Formentor? Let’s take a closer look…

Starting with the design, there are a couple of updates, most notably at the front. There’s an all-new grille, while the front air intakes have also been updated. The long, fang-like DRLs have also been removed, instead replaced with smaller, 3-line lights. Along the side, there are new alloy wheels, and around the back, the badge has been replaced with the brand’s name in the full-length light bar. 

On the inside, Peugeot has kept the interior more or less the same as the previous iteration. There are some new materials high up on the dash, new kick plates have been added, and new dial screen graphics have been added. Otherwise, it’s the same as before, which means there’s a laggy and unintuitive infotainment screen, a decent amount of storage, and a customisable screen for your key features and functions. 

There are two engine options available for the 408, and they’re both hybrids. The entry-level is a 1.2-litre petrol coupled with a self-charging motor, and this produces 145hp. There’s also a 1.6-litre plug-in hybrid, and this produces 240hp. Both cars are front wheel drive. 

The entry-level 408 starts from around £32,000, rising all the way to £38,760 for the range-topper. So is it worth it? You’ll need to stick with Mat to see for yourself!

Explore new & used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-07-07

Mat's Peugeot 408 choice: https://bit.ly/Mats-Choice-070726

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

⭐ BREAK 75 & GOLF ENTERTAINMENT

Break 75 has become one of YouTube’s biggest golf series.
You’ll see:

• Full 18-hole course vlogs
• Course management strategies
• Matches against tour players and creators
• Pressure moments, birdie runs and disaster holes
• Some of the world’s best golf courses
• Real, relatable golf

I also play matches and challenges with some of the biggest names in golf, including:

• Bryson DeChambeau
• Peter Finch
• Good Good
• GM Golf
• Bob Does Sports
• BustaJack
• Grant Horvat
• Me and My Golf
• Danny Maude

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

⭐ TRAVEL, COURSE VLOGS & LIV CONTENT

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

⭐ THE GOAL

To make golf more fun, more accessible and help you become a better, more confident, happier golfer — whether you’re breaking 100 or trying to break par. Be sure to subscribe to LIV Golf here:

videos.title: Finansdagen 2025 – Omställning i en föränderlig värld
videos.summary_source_text:
Finansdagen 2025 – Omställning i en föränderlig värld Välkommen till Finansdagen 2025! Här får du ta del av insiktsfulla föreläsningar, inspirerande samtal och aktuella analyser om finansbranschens möjligheter och utmaningar. I årets upplaga samlas ledande experter, branschprofiler och beslutsfattare för att dela sina perspektiv kring ekonomi, hållbarhet och framtidens finansiella landskap. Oavsett om du är yrkesverksam inom finans, student eller bara nyfiken på de senaste trenderna, ger Finansdagen 2025 dig kunskap och inspiration att ta nästa steg. 

Dagen leds av moderatorerna Hanne Setane, Hampus Engzell och Måns Liljenlov, PwC Sverige. Keynote Michael Green, vd Handelsbanken, intervjuas av Måns Liljenlov, PwC. Hur påverkas branschen av olika omvärlstrender, geopolitik, makroekonomis ka läget och hållbarhetsagendan? Panelsamtal med Frida Wallnor, politisk redaktör DI, Jens Magnusson, chefsekonom SEB, Emilia Högquist, hållbarhetschef Finansinspektionen. 

Se fler avsnitt från dagen och ladda ned material på vår webbplats https://www.pwc.se/finansdagen. Missa inte chansen att ta del av de viktigaste samtalen inom finansvärlden!

videos.title: Finansdagen 2025 – Omställning: Hållbarhet
videos.summary_source_text:
Finansdagen 2025 – Omställning: Hållbarhet Välkommen till Finansdagen 2025! Här får du ta del av insiktsfulla föreläsningar, inspirerande samtal och aktuella analyser om finansbranschens möjligheter och utmaningar. I årets upplaga samlas ledande experter, branschprofiler och beslutsfattare för att dela sina perspektiv kring ekonomi, hållbarhet och framtidens finansiella landskap. Oavsett om du är yrkesverksam inom finans, student eller bara nyfiken på de senaste trenderna, ger Finansdagen 2025 dig kunskap och inspiration att ta nästa steg. 

Hur kan finansbranschen bidra till den hållbara omställningen framåt? Investeringar i social impact, forskning inom planetära gränser. Varför lyckas en del och andra inte? Hur ställer vi om i praktiken och vilken roll har ledning och styrelse för innovation och omställning? 

Panelsamtal med Jenny Carenco, partner Utfallfonden, Lisen Schultz, Associate Professor in Sustainability Science, Dep. Director Stockholm Resilience Centre, Klara Adolphson, Styrelseordförande Våra barns klimat och CEO Innovation Leadership Group.

Se fler avsnitt från dagen och ladda ned material på vår webbplats https://www.pwc.se/finansdagen. Missa inte chansen att ta del av de viktigaste samtalen inom finansvärlden! 

Tack för att du tittar – glöm inte att prenumerera på kanalen!

videos.title: 06.07.26 - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
06.07.26 - RIFT Presentation RIFT is a modern trading platform that combines automated trading, manual trading, futures trading, and educational tools into one ecosystem.

Designed for both beginners and experienced traders, RIFT aims to simplify access to financial markets while providing users with tools that can help them participate more efficiently.

One of the key features of RIFT is its Auto Trading Engine, which allows users to allocate capital into automated trading strategies designed to operate without the need for constant monitoring. This enables participants to remain active in the markets without spending hours each day analyzing charts or placing trades manually.

For users who prefer direct market participation, RIFT also offers Manual Trading and Futures Trading functionality. This gives traders the flexibility to execute their own strategies, manage positions, and take advantage of market opportunities in real time.

RIFT is built around a non-custodial philosophy, meaning users maintain control of their own funds rather than depositing them into a centralized company account. This approach aligns with the growing demand for greater transparency, security, and user control within the digital asset industry.

Beyond trading, RIFT focuses heavily on education. Through training sessions, community support, and ongoing learning resources, users can develop a deeper understanding of trading, risk management, market psychology, and financial technology.

The platform also incorporates modern technologies such as AI-driven infrastructure, advanced execution systems, copy trading capabilities, and future integrations designed to create a more connected and intelligent trading environment.

Whether you are completely new to trading, looking for automated solutions, interested in futures markets, or seeking to expand your financial knowledge, RIFT aims to provide a complete ecosystem where users can learn, participate, and grow.

Key Features

• Auto Trading Engine
• Manual Trading
• Futures Trading
• Non-Custodial Infrastructure
• AI-Powered Trading Tools
• Copy Trading Features
• Educational Resources & Training
• Community Support
• Advanced Market Access
• Designed for Beginners and Experienced Traders

RIFT is built around a simple vision: making modern trading technology more accessible while helping users participate in financial markets through a combination of automation, education, and innovative infrastructure. videos.title: Fix The Stuck Move In Your Downswing – Blue Brick
videos.summary_source_text:
Fix The Stuck Move In Your Downswing – Blue Brick Click link in bio for lowest price on the Blue Brick. This one's for better players who already know how to shallow the club and hit a draw but still fight getting "stuck" in the downswing.

You'll learn the difference between the stuck move (butt end riding up, shaft dropping under) and the correct move (butt end working down and in, shaft kicking on top) — the same move you see pros like Tommy Fleetwood drill.

Once you feel this, you'll be able to release the club as hard as you want without any fear of hooking it — the ball will go straight or fade.

We used the Blue Brick to help groove the correct path and shaft angle during the drill.

Like this video if this helped, and grab your own Blue Brick using the link in my bio.

videos.title: ROSTER UPDATES! Carolina Hurricanes Heading to Arbitration & Sign a New Netminder
videos.summary_source_text:
ROSTER UPDATES! Carolina Hurricanes Heading to Arbitration & Sign a New Netminder As we start a new week in the offseason, what's the latest news surrounding the Carolina Hurricanes? Between arbitration, a new signing, and a way too early roster projection, how are things shaping up in Raleigh? On the Monday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on the news of Ronan Seeley electing for arbitration. Earlier in the week, the Hurricanes gave Seeley a qualifying offer, along with six other players. However, he decided to go to arbitration on Sunday. What could that look like for both sides? Secondly, the Hurricanes signed netminder Zach Sawchenko to a one-year, two-way deal on Saturday. What does it mean for both sides as the Hurricanes look to shore up the depth in the crease? Finally, Zach gives his way too early roster line projection ahead of the 2026-27 season. While we are only a few days into free agency and following the draft, what could the team look like? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: Vad kostar mat för en familj egentligen?
videos.summary_source_text:
Vad kostar mat för en familj egentligen? Konsumentverket räknar med runt 2 800 kr i matkostnad per vuxen och månad, och kanske tiotusen för en familj med två vuxna och två barn. Min egen erfarenhet stämmer sällan med den ramen.

En del av förklaringen är våra egna val, som att handla på en dyrare butik för bekvämlighetens skull. Samtidigt hör jag liknande siffror från många i min omgivning, ofta 15 000 till 20 000 i månaden för en familj.

Det väcker en intressant fråga om hur väl schablonerna speglar verkliga hushåll. Jag är nyfiken på hur det ser ut för andra.

Mer på rikatillsammans.se Konsumentverket räknar med runt 2 800 kr i matkostnad per vuxen och månad, och kanske tiotusen för en familj med två vuxna och två barn. Min egen erfarenhet stämmer sällan med den ramen.

videos.title: THE FIRST SIGN YOUR PROSTATE IS GETTING BIGGER | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
THE FIRST SIGN YOUR PROSTATE IS GETTING BIGGER | Dr. Mandell If you're over the age of 45, understanding the early warning signs of an enlarged prostate can make a significant difference in your long-term health and quality of life. In this video, Dr. Alan Mandell explains the first subtle signs your prostate may be getting bigger, why these changes occur, and what you can do naturally to help support healthy prostate and bladder function. You'll learn how the prostate surrounds the urethra, why hormonal changes involving dihydrotestosterone (DHT) and chronic inflammation can contribute to enlargement, and why symptoms such as a weaker urine stream, difficulty getting started, incomplete bladder emptying, frequent urination, urgency, dribbling, and waking up at night should never be ignored. I also discuss evidence-based lifestyle changes, including maintaining a healthy weight, exercising regularly, reducing chronic inflammation, staying properly hydrated during the day while limiting fluids before bedtime, improving bowel regularity, and eating an anti-inflammatory diet rich in colorful vegetables, tomatoes, cruciferous vegetables like broccoli and Brussels sprouts, berries, healthy fats, nuts, seeds, and omega-3-rich fish. We also review several natural supplements that have been studied for prostate health, including saw palmetto, an herbal extract from the berries of the saw palmetto palm that may help reduce urinary symptoms in some men; beta-sitosterol, a plant sterol found in vegetables, nuts, seeds, and fruits that has been shown in studies to improve urinary flow and reduce residual urine; pygeum africanum, an extract from the bark of the African cherry tree that may help decrease nighttime urination and improve urinary comfort; pumpkin seed extract, which contains beneficial phytosterols and antioxidants that may support normal prostate function; and rye grass pollen extract, which has shown potential in improving urinary symptoms and overall quality of life in some men with benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH). While these supplements may provide modest benefits for certain individuals, they should not replace medical evaluation or treatment when symptoms are persistent or worsening. If you experience blood in your urine, severe pain, fever, an inability to urinate, unexplained weight loss, or rapidly worsening urinary symptoms, seek medical attention immediately. Taking action early through healthy lifestyle choices and proper medical care can help support a healthier prostate, improve bladder function, reduce nighttime urination, and allow you to enjoy a healthier, more active life. The Dallas Mavericks’ roster appears to be taking shape. Currently, there are 13 players under contract on the team, from Cooper Flagg and Kyrie Irving on down to rookies Morez Johnson Jr. and Sergio de Larrea. The DLLS Mavs crew takes stock of the team right now, identifies areas for improvement, and provides more thoughts on moves from around the league during a busy Fourth of July weekend. Plus, Marc Stein joins with the latest on the Mavs’ new additions to Dusty May’s coaching staff: Willie Green and Garrett Temple.

videos.title: Leo Carlsson Signs $90M Flyers Offer Sheet + Ducks Under Pressure ft. Steve Werier & Emerson Etem
videos.summary_source_text:
Leo Carlsson Signs $90M Flyers Offer Sheet + Ducks Under Pressure ft. Steve Werier & Emerson Etem On today’s episode of The Sheet with Jeff Marek, Jeff is joined by former NHL executive Steve Werier for a deep dive into the changing world of NHL player contracts, roster building, and the future of team management. They break down the fallout from Leo Carlsson’s massive $90 million offer sheet with the Philadelphia Flyers, what it means for the Anaheim Ducks, and how it could reshape the way NHL teams approach restricted free agency moving forward. Jeff and Steve discuss the rise of offer sheets, potential loopholes in the system, salary cap strategy, market inefficiencies, contract negotiations, and where front offices may look for advantages in the next era of the NHL. Later, former Anaheim Duck and current Ducks color analyst Emerson Etem joins the show to give his perspective on the Carlsson situation, Pavel Mintyukov’s new contract with Anaheim, the Ducks’ current salary cap challenges, their young core, and what comes next as the franchise navigates a crucial offseason. Don’t miss another packed episode of The Sheet covering the biggest NHL news, rumors, contracts, and analysis from around the league.

#TheSheet #NHL #Hockey #AnaheimDucks #Flyers #NHLOffseason #NHLFreeAgency #NHLRumors

Later, former Anaheim Duck and current Ducks color analyst Emerson Etem joins the show to give his perspective on the Carlsson situation, Pavel Mintyukov’s new contract with Anaheim, the Ducks’ current salary cap challenges, their young core, and what comes next as the franchise navigates a crucial offseason. Don’t miss another packed episode of The Sheet covering the biggest NHL news, rumors, contracts, and analysis from around the league.

videos.summary_source_text:
How to Use Claude Fable 5: Complete Beginner's Guide (2026) Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Claude Fable 5 is the most capable AI model Anthropic has ever released publicly — and it can quietly swap itself out for an older, weaker model mid-conversation without you realizing the practical impact. In this video, I break down what Fable 5 actually is, the dual-model architecture it shares with Claude Mythos 5, the classifier system that's earned it the nickname "Feeble 5," the unprecedented U.S. government shutdown that took it offline for two weeks, and the 30-day data retention clause that made Microsoft pause.

We cover the real benchmark numbers (SWE-Bench Pro, FrontierCode Diamond, AI2 Intelligence Index), Simon Willison's hands-on testing, the Stripe 50-million-line migration claim, pricing versus Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5 Pro, and the honest limitations independent testers found — including hallucination rates that may be worse than the model it replaced.

By the end, you'll know exactly whether Fable 5 is worth your money — or whether Opus 4.8 at half the price is the smarter buy.

videos.title: Why "Natural Flavors" Doesn't Mean Healthy
videos.summary_source_text:
Why "Natural Flavors" Doesn't Mean Healthy The word "natural" has become one of the most misunderstood terms on a food label. Natural flavors don't have to disclose exactly what they contain, and "natural" doesn't automatically mean healthy. That's why it's important to look beyond the marketing on the front of the package and start reading the ingredient list. At the end of the day, the choices you make are the ones that shape your health.

The founder and Chief Medical Officer of Aristotle Education and has been practicing medicine in Central Florida for over 35 years. As a consultant cardiologist with a large diversified inpatient and outpatient practice he is noted for his passions for teaching and illuminating prevention for cardiovascular disease. videos.title: 12 Exercises That Completely Change The Human Body
videos.summary_source_text:
12 Exercises That Completely Change The Human Body 22 Days To Double Your Pullups - http://athleanx.com/morepullups
Subscribe to this channel here - http://bit.ly/2b0coMW

In this video, I’m breaking down the 12 exercises that completely change the human body and are great at helping people who do them build muscle, increase strength, improve athletic performance, and create a body that is more resilient to injury. These are not random exercises. These are fundamental movement patterns that train the entire body, from your legs, glutes, back, chest, shoulders, arms, and core to the smaller stabilizing muscles that often get overlooked.

The best exercises for building muscle are not always the flashiest. They are the ones that allow you to progressively overload, move through the right ranges of motion, train multiple muscle groups, and develop strength that carries over to everything else you do. That is why exercises like squats, deadlifts, bench press, pullups, pushups, rows, lunges, curls, triceps extensions, face pulls, and banded external rotations all belong in a complete workout plan.

I start with the squat, one of the most important lower body exercises for building stronger legs, glutes, hips, and overall lower body strength. If traditional squats bother your knees or low back, I’ll show you exercise variations like the Bulgarian split squat and box squat to help you train the same movement pattern with less discomfort.

Next is the deadlift, one of the best strength exercises for training the posterior chain, including the glutes, hamstrings, spinal erectors, and upper back. The key is learning how to hinge properly. Whether you pull from the floor, elevate the bar, or use a trap bar deadlift variation, mastering the hinge is one of the best things you can do to build strength and protect your lower back.

The bench press earns its place as one of the best upper body exercises for building the chest, shoulders, and triceps. If shoulder pain gets in the way, I’ll show you how a controlled dumbbell press can help restore stability while still allowing you to train the horizontal pressing pattern effectively.

Pullups are another must-have exercise. They are one of the best bodyweight back exercises and one of the most effective ways to build your lats, upper back, grip strength, and relative body strength. Whether you use weighted pullups, assisted pullups, or band-assisted pullups, the movement belongs in your program. If you are trying to improve your pullups, the goal is to choose the version that matches your current strength and progress it from there.

Pushups also make the list, but only if they are challenging enough. If you can easily do 30, 40, or 50 reps, it is time to choose a harder pushup variation so you can continue building strength and muscle. The right pushup variation can challenge the chest, shoulders, triceps, and core with nothing more than your bodyweight.

I’ll also cover the importance of rows, lunges, barbell curls, triceps extensions, face pulls, and banded external rotations. Rows help balance out your pressing and build the upper back. Lunges train single leg strength, stability, and athletic control. Barbell curls and triceps extensions complete the development of the arms. Face pulls and banded external rotations help strengthen the rotator cuff, rear delts, and upper back muscles that keep your shoulders healthier over the long term.

A complete workout program should not just be a collection of random exercises. It should include the best exercises for strength, muscle growth, athletic movement, injury prevention, joint stability, and total body development. These 12 exercises check all of those boxes. They train the major movement patterns your body needs, they can be modified for different ability levels, and they give you a foundation that almost every lifter can benefit from.

If you want to build muscle, get stronger, improve your pullups, and train with a smarter plan, make sure these exercises are represented in your program. If you want a complete workout plan that includes these must-do exercises and organizes them into a step-by-step program based on your goals, head to athleanx.com using the link above and check out 22 Days To Double Your Pullups.

For more videos on the best exercises for building muscle, the best strength exercises, bodyweight exercises, corrective exercises, pullup workouts, and complete workout plans, subscribe to ATHLEAN-X here on YouTube and turn on notifications so you never miss a new video. 22 Days To Double Your Pullups - http://athleanx.com/morepullups
Subscribe to this channel here - http://bit.ly/2b0coMW

In this video, I’m breaking down the 12 exercises that completely change the human body and are great at helping people who do them build muscle, increase strength, improve athletic performance, and create a body that is more resilient to injury. These are not random exercises. These are fundamental movement patterns that train the entire body, from your legs, glutes, back, chest, shoulders, arms, and core to the smaller stabilizing muscles that often get overlooked.

The best exercises for building muscle are not always the flashiest. They are the ones that allow you to progressively overload, move through the right ranges of motion, train multiple muscle groups, and develop strength that carries over to everything else you do. That is why exercises like squats, deadlifts, bench press, pullups, pushups, rows, lunges, curls, triceps extensions, face pulls, and banded external rotations all belong in a complete workout plan.

I start with the squat, one of the most important lower body exercises for building stronger legs, glutes, hips, and overall lower body strength. If traditional squats bother your knees or low back, I’ll show you exercise variations like the Bulgarian split squat and box squat to help you train the same movement pattern with less discomfort.

Next is the deadlift, one of the best strength exercises for training the posterior chain, including the glutes, hamstrings, spinal erectors, and upper back. The key is learning how to hinge properly. Whether you pull from the floor, elevate the bar, or use a trap bar deadlift variation, mastering the hinge is one of the best things you can do to build strength and protect your lower back.

The bench press earns its place as one of the best upper body exercises for building the chest, shoulders, and triceps. If shoulder pain gets in the way, I’ll show you how a controlled dumbbell press can help restore stability while still allowing you to train the horizontal pressing pattern effectively.

Pullups are another must-have exercise. They are one of the best bodyweight back exercises and one of the most effective ways to build your lats, upper back, grip strength, and relative body strength. Whether you use weighted pullups, assisted pullups, or band-assisted pullups, the movement belongs in your program. If you are trying to improve your pullups, the goal is to choose the version that matches your current strength and progress it from there.

Pushups also make the list, but only if they are challenging enough. If you can easily do 30, 40, or 50 reps, it is time to choose a harder pushup variation so you can continue building strength and muscle. The right pushup variation can challenge the chest, shoulders, triceps, and core with nothing more than your bodyweight.

I’ll also cover the importance of rows, lunges, barbell curls, triceps extensions, face pulls, and banded external rotations. Rows help balance out your pressing and build the upper back. Lunges train single leg strength, stability, and athletic control. Barbell curls and triceps extensions complete the development of the arms. Face pulls and banded external rotations help strengthen the rotator cuff, rear delts, and upper back muscles that keep your shoulders healthier over the long term.

A complete workout program should not just be a collection of random exercises. It should include the best exercises for strength, muscle growth, athletic movement, injury prevention, joint stability, and total body development. These 12 exercises check all of those boxes. They train the major movement patterns your body needs, they can be modified for different ability levels, and they give you a foundation that almost every lifter can benefit from.

If you want to build muscle, get stronger, improve your pullups, and train with a smarter plan, make sure these exercises are represented in your program. If you want a complete workout plan that includes these must-do exercises and organizes them into a step-by-step program based on your goals, head to athleanx.com using the link above and check out 22 Days To Double Your Pullups.

videos.title: Gemini 4 Isn't Real Yet Here's What Google Actually Shipped
videos.summary_source_text:
Gemini 4 Isn't Real Yet Here's What Google Actually Shipped Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/

Here's a detailed, SEO-optimized YouTube description for this script:

Gemini 4 Doesn't Exist Yet — Here's What Google Actually Released
Everyone's talking about Gemini 4 like it's already here. It's not. In this video, I spent two weeks digging through DeepMind's actual model cards, safety reports, and every "leak" floating around online — and what I found is that the real story isn't Gemini 4. It's what Google quietly shipped instead.
We break down exactly what's confirmed, what's speculation, and what it means for how you actually use AI day to day.
In this video, you'll learn:

Google's real Gemini release timeline — from Gemini 1.0 in late 2023 through Gemini 3.5 Flash and Gemini Omni in 2026
Why "Gemini 4" isn't officially announced, and where the rumors are actually coming from
What Gemini 3.5 Flash can really do: multimodal generation, a 1M+ token context window, and near real-time speed
How Gemini 3.5 performs on agentic benchmarks like Terminal-Bench 2.1, and how it uses Google's Antigravity platform for tool use and multi-step tasks

A head-to-head comparison: Gemini 3.5 vs GPT-4o vs Llama 4 Maverick vs Claude — speed, context length, price, and tooling differences that actually matter


What a real Gemini 4 would likely include based on Google's actual capability trends: longer context, deeper reasoning, richer agents, and expanded multimodal features

How to access Gemini today — API pricing, AI Studio, Vertex AI, Google AI Pro/Ultra plans, and on-device Gemma 4 Edge models
The privacy and safety measures actually built into the Gemini stack, including SynthID watermarking and Frontier Safety evaluations
Why the "Gemini 4 will be AGI" headlines don't hold up against DeepMind's own published criteria

The real-world impact already happening — research, coding, customer service automation, and content creation — plus the risks of over-relying on these tools

00:02:13 What Gemini 3.5 Flash Can Actually Do

00:06:05 What to Realistically Expect Next
00:07:02 How to Use Gemini Today
00:07:51 Privacy & Safety in Practice
00:08:42 Gemini's Limitations & the AGI Question
00:09:42 Real-World Impact
00:10:25 Final Thoughts


If this breakdown saved you from another hype cycle, drop a like and subscribe with notifications on — the moment real Gemini 4 news drops, it'll be covered here first.

videos.title: Will the Ducks Match Leo Carlsson? + Arbitration Drama | DFO Rundown
videos.summary_source_text:
Will the Ducks Match Leo Carlsson? + Arbitration Drama | DFO Rundown The NHL offseason just took another wild turn.

Jason Gregor and Jeff Marek break down the blockbuster offer sheet submitted to Leo Carlsson and debate whether the Anaheim Ducks should match or take the draft pick compensation. What does Pavel Mintyukov's new contract mean for Anaheim's cap situation, and how does Cutter Gauthier fit into the equation?

The guys also discuss Utah's decision on Barrett Hayton, why New Jersey's offer sheet was a smart piece of business, and the ripple effects these moves could have around the NHL.

Later, they analyze the New Jersey Devils' offseason, the Toronto Maple Leafs' roster overhaul, the best remaining unrestricted free agents led by Anthony Mantha, and the latest on Shane Wright's future in Seattle.

Finally, they break down the NHL's arbitration filings, including Jason Robertson, Cole Perfetti, Trevor Zegras, Jamie Drysdale, Connor McMichael, Kirby Dach and more.

If you're looking for the latest NHL trade rumors, offer sheet news, free agency updates and offseason analysis, this episode has you covered.

(Filmed July 5th, 7p.m. ET)

videos.summary_source_text:
I Took on REAL Open Qualifying I took on the ultimate golf challenge: final stage Open Qualifying at Burnham and Berrow golf club, playing the exact same championship tees and pins just one day after the pros tried to earn their spots in The Open. In this brutal links golf match against the elements, you’ll learn how to play golf in tough conditions, survive intense wind, and manage your game when level par feels like an impossible score. Watch to see how a professional coach grinds it out against tour pro benchmarks!

Mac Mini for Local AI: Worth It? Try Tank for yourself! Join the community 👉 https://mrc.fm/cmc

I've been itching to see how a regular Mac mini really holds up as a local AI machine, so in this stream I'm putting one through its paces live. I'll run a few local LLMs, push some inference, and see whether that unified memory earns its keep for the sort of self-hosted, local-first setup I love. We'll get into RAM tiers, real-world token speeds, and whether this compact little box is the one worth buying or whether you're better off holding out for what's coming. Bring your questions and your prompts and we'll test them together.

The Top 5 Foods to Lower Cholesterol Naturally Your body makes cholesterol on its own, so lowering cholesterol isn’t just about cutting certain foods out of your diet. Consuming foods that lower cholesterol can be more important than focusing on foods to avoid. 

🌟 Take the 2-minute Health Lever Quiz: https://drbrg.co/3S5fJ4F

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

Five foods that support healthy cholesterol levels:
• Arugula or romaine lettuce
• Walnuts
• Extra-virgin olive oil
• Avocados
• Nutritional yeast

These foods help support healthy cholesterol levels, arterial health, and healthy LDL and HDL balance.

#cholesterol #lowercholesterol #ldlcholesterol #hdlcholesterol #hearthealth #oliveoil #avocado #walnuts #nutrition #healthyfoods

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

Break 75 has become one of YouTube’s biggest golf series.
You’ll see:

• Full 18-hole course vlogs

• Matches against tour players and creators

• Some of the world’s best golf courses

• Bryson DeChambeau
• Peter Finch

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

To make golf more fun, more accessible and help you become a better, more confident, happier golfer — whether you’re breaking 100 or trying to break par. Be sure to subscribe to LIV Golf here:

videos.title: Could the ball REPLACE the Pro V1 🤔
videos.summary_source_text:
Could the ball REPLACE the Pro V1 🤔 Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

videos.title: You’ve Been Misled About How To Start The Golf Swing
videos.summary_source_text:
You’ve Been Misled About How To Start The Golf Swing In this golf lesson, Eric Cogorno and JT Thomas explain why low and slow destroys your golf takeaway — and how early wrist hinge fixes it.

⏱️ Timestamps:
🔍 0:00 - Eric Cogorno and JT Thomas: why low and slow hurts your golf takeaway
💡 1:49 - Why low and slow causes golfers to move everything together
🎯 3:00 - The bad version: what overdoing low and slow actually looks like
📐 3:46 - Adam Scott and Nelly Korda: hands below hip line, club head above
💪 4:34 - The lead hand drill for getting early wrist hinge in the backswing
⛳ 5:53 - How early wrist hinge gets the club on plane from down the line
🔄 8:48 - How the body pivot and hand path create width without losing hinge
🏆 12:15 - How getting the club head moving sooner leads to more solid contact

⭐ OUR AWESOME PARTNERS AND DISCOUNT CODES ⭐
🏌️ Takomo Golf Clubs - https://takomogolf.com
👕 Bobby Jones Apparel: https://bobbyjones.com/COGORNO

⛳ theHanger Training Aid - https://www.hangergolf.com/?sca_ref=944104.HqM9GYcX2I (Code: COGORNO for 15% OFF)

If you've been told to keep the club low and slow your whole golfing life, this video is going to change how you start your golf swing. Eric Cogorno and JT Thomas break down why low and slow causes golfers to move the body and club together — and why that makes everything harder later in the swing. The fix is early wrist hinge in the backswing: getting the club head moving sooner so you can create separation in transition, stay on plane, and hit the ball more solid. Watch how Adam Scott and Nelly Korda use wrist hinge in the golf swing to get the club head above the hip line early — and how the lead hand drill gives you the same feel in minutes. If you leave this video without trying the lead hand drill, you're leaving solid contact on the table.

What You'll Learn:
• Why low and slow in the golf takeaway causes more problems than it solves
• How moving the body and club together early leads to a worse transition and less solid contact
• The correct wrist hinge in the backswing: hands below hip line, club head above
• How Adam Scott and Nelly Korda use early wrist hinge in the golf swing to stay on plane
• The lead hand drill for training wrist hinge in the backswing from day one
• How early wrist hinge gets the club on plane from down the line
• How the body pivot and hand path create width without sacrificing wrist hinge in the golf swing
• Why getting the club head moving sooner leads to better iron compression and more distance

• Why getting the club head moving sooner leads to better iron compression and more distance

videos.title: Leclerc 'Transforms The Narrative' | British GP Review | F1 Nation Podcast
videos.summary_source_text:
Leclerc 'Transforms The Narrative' | British GP Review | F1 Nation Podcast Leclerc 'Transforms The Narrative' | British GP Review | F1 Nation Podcast

Tom Clarkson is joined by F1TV commentary duo, former F1 driver Jolyon Palmer and lead commentator Alex Jacques, to reflect on a dramatic British Grand Prix.

Charles Leclerc capitalised on more misfortune for championship leader Kimi Antonelli to take his first victory since Austin in 2024. After such a difficult run of races, why was he back to his best and how significant is this win for Charles?

Reliability issues mean Antonelli has seen his championship lead cut from 66 points to just 25 over the last three rounds, but is he leaving Silverstone happier than George Russell despite the Brit taking home P2 and closing the gap to Kimi? Jolyon, Alex and Tom share their thoughts on George’s continuing struggles against his teammate and the team radio message from Mercedes Team Principal Toto Wolff at the end of the race. 

Plus, the guys discuss why Lewis Hamilton might be ‘scratching his head’ despite securing a fourth podium finish of the season and gaining more ground on Antonelli.


0:00 - Kimi loses more points
7:08 - Leclerc 'back to his best'
13:47 - Russell speaks his mind
24:09 - Highs and lows for Hamilton
28:26 - Verstappen spins out
35:03: Hadjar, Lawson, Gabi or Leclerc for DOTD?

videos.title: Final Round DRAMA in Munich | 2026 BMW International Open
videos.summary_source_text:
Final Round DRAMA in Munich | 2026 BMW International Open Watch the drama unfold in the Back 9 on Sunday, presented by Nexo as Michael Hollick eagled the 72nd hole to snatch his maiden DP World Tour title and deny fellow South African Hennie Du Plessis at the 2026 BMW International Open.

The 39-year-old rookie shared the overnight lead heading into Sunday's final round and was briefly three shots behind his compatriot with two holes to play at Golfclub München Eichenried. Hollick becomes the 5th South African to win on Tour this season.

► SUBSCRIBE: http://et.golf/Subscribe 

The European Swing comes to a climax this week as the DP World Tour arrives in Germany. After five counting events in as many countries, the European Swing - the fourth and penultimate of the five Global Swings - draws to a close in Germany. Dan Brown will aim to do something never achieved before in the history of the BMW International Open: successfully defend the title. Last year, the Englishman won his second DP World Tour crown, carding a bogey-free final round of 66 as he fought off countryman Jordan Smith to win by two shots. Headlined by Race to Dubai leader Patrick Reed, a strong global array of talent has assembled as players representing 31 nationalities are set to feature. The American is joined by this season's fellow two-time DP World Tour winners Schaper, Casey Jarvis and Chacarra in teeing it up. Reed is one of four Major champions on show, joined by fellow Green Jacket winners Danny Willett and Sergio Garcia along with Martin Kaymer.

Welcome to the Official YouTube channel of the DP World Tour. Every week you can find extended tournament highlights and individual player highlights from the likes of Rory McIlroy, Justin Rose and Tommy Fleetwood. This is also the home of the 14 Club Challenge, Little Interviews, Fastest Hole and many more challenges, pranks and world record attempts featuring the biggest golfers in the world.

videos.title: 472  Vår parterapeut Tommy Waad om tillit, pengabråk och tysta män
videos.summary_source_text:
472  Vår parterapeut Tommy Waad om tillit, pengabråk och tysta män Varför bråkar vi egentligen om pengar? Parterapeuten Tommy Waad menar att det nästan aldrig handlar om kronorna, utan om tillit och om tvivlet på att den andra finns kvar. 

Åtta år efter vårt första besök hos Tommy behövde vi i våras göra ett återbesök eftersom vi hade en konflikt vi inte fick rätt på. Hans initiala analys var att det handlade om brist på tillit. Det kändes jättekonstigt eftersom vi ändå varit ihop sedan 2003. Men ju längre vi kom i samtalen desto tydligare blev det att ingen av oss hade tillit till varandra egentligen. 

Så här ett antal månader senare bjöd vi in Tommy till podden för att prata om det vi varit med om, att det är vanligare än man tror samtidigt som han svarade på många av era frågor. Några av ämnena vi tar upp veckans avsnitt är: 

• Varför pengabråk nästan aldrig handlar om kronorna, utan om tillit
• De tre typerna av par: de som bråkar, de som tiger och de frustrerade
• Varför frustration är ett friskhetstecken och likgiltigheten den verkliga faran
• Den tyste mannen och mönstren som får oss att dra oss undan
• Varför tilliten måste utmanas och inte växer av sig själv
• Varningstecknen på att en relation är på väg åt fel håll
• Att klaga utan att kräva en lösning, för varje klagan rymmer en önskan
• När parterapi passar och hur du väljer rätt terapeut
• Det enda du kan göra redan i kväll för din partner

Vi hoppas att du gillar avsnittet,
Jan, Caroline och Tommy

Innehållsförteckning och kapitel:
====
00:00:00 – Introduktion till avsnittet
00:03:22 – Åtta år senare hos Tommy: bakgrunden och känslomässig närhet
00:06:01 – Tre typer av par: de som bråkar, de som tiger och de frustrerade
00:08:10 – Varför män håller låg profil: könsroller och barndomsmönster
00:11:40 – Omedvetna mönster och den tyste mannen i skuggvärlden
00:18:17 – Lucky Luke som mansbild och Carolines insikt om sårbarhet
00:22:40 – Frustrationen som kärlek: vad den frustrerade egentligen signalerar
00:25:35 – Tillit som grundsten: hur tvivlet blockerar allt i relationen
00:28:00 – Varma ord och bekräftelse blockeras av tvivlet på den andre
00:33:26 – Positivt beroende: anknytningsteorin om att kunna räkna med varandra
00:38:34 – Hur man bryter mönstret: utmana tilliten även när det känns svårt
00:43:45 – Att klaga utan att kräva lösning: behovet av att bli lyssnad på
00:46:21 – Barn och hundar före partner: varför Jan lyssnar annorlunda
00:49:54 – Självkärlek och anknytning: bilden av sig själv och bilden av andra
00:54:55 – Varningstecken: hur man fångar att relationen är på väg utför
00:57:43 – Varför separationer ofta kommer som en chock för en av parterna
00:59:23 – Nyfikenheten på varandra: nyckeln som håller kontakten vid liv
01:01:05 – När par söker hjälp: småbarnsåren, fyrtioårskrisen och tomboet
01:04:28 – Parrelationen som tredje part: Jan, Caroline och relationen var för sig
01:10:43 – Hur man uttrycker behov och undviker obalans i det emotionella ansvaret
01:14:19 – Fördomen att parterapi bara löser logistik, inte kärleken
01:22:13 – Pengabråk och gemensam ekonomi: allt handlar om tillit
01:33:35 – Livsfaser: framgångsrik kvinna, pension och styvbarn i nya relationer
01:38:03 – Icks, röda flaggor och positiv acceptans för en partners skavanker
01:41:33 – När parterapi passar och hur man väljer rätt terapeut
01:49:20 – Gottman och Sue Johnson: forskning om vad som håller ihop par
01:52:04 – Sammanfattning: ta hand om din partner på det sätt som faktiskt tas emot

Länkar från avsnittet:
====
Tommys hemsida:  https://parterapiinstitutet.se/
Pengar och relationer: https://rikatillsammans.se/ett-rikare-liv/pengar-och-relationer/
Emotionella sidan av pengar: https://rikatillsammans.se/ett-rikare-liv/emotionella-sidan-av-pengar/
Våra samtal om pengar är ju sjukt trista: https://rikatillsammans.se/avsnitt/vara-samtal-om-pengar-ar-ju-sjukt-trista/
Frågor att ställa sig och sin partner i slutet av året: https://rikatillsammans.se/avsnitt/fragor-att-stalla-sig-och-sin-partner-i-slutet-av-aret/

Stötta oss på Patreon:
https://www.patreon.com/join/rikatillsammans

Diskutera avsnittet i forumet:
https://rikatillsammans.se/forum/t/123617

Prenumerera på nyhetsbrevet:
https://rikatillsammans.se/nyhetsbrev Varför bråkar vi egentligen om pengar? Parterapeuten Tommy Waad menar att det nästan aldrig handlar om kronorna, utan om tillit och om tvivlet på att den andra finns kvar. 

Åtta år efter vårt första besök hos Tommy behövde vi i våras göra ett återbesök eftersom vi hade en konflikt vi inte fick rätt på. Hans initiala analys var att det handlade om brist på tillit. Det kändes jättekonstigt eftersom vi ändå varit ihop sedan 2003. Men ju längre vi kom i samtalen desto tydligare blev det att ingen av oss hade tillit till varandra egentligen. 

Så här ett antal månader senare bjöd vi in Tommy till podden för att prata om det vi varit med om, att det är vanligare än man tror samtidigt som han svarade på många av era frågor. Några av ämnena vi tar upp veckans avsnitt är: 

• Varför pengabråk nästan aldrig handlar om kronorna, utan om tillit
• De tre typerna av par: de som bråkar, de som tiger och de frustrerade
• Varför frustration är ett friskhetstecken och likgiltigheten den verkliga faran
• Den tyste mannen och mönstren som får oss att dra oss undan
• Varför tilliten måste utmanas och inte växer av sig själv
• Varningstecknen på att en relation är på väg åt fel håll
• Att klaga utan att kräva en lösning, för varje klagan rymmer en önskan
• När parterapi passar och hur du väljer rätt terapeut
• Det enda du kan göra redan i kväll för din partner

Vi hoppas att du gillar avsnittet,
Jan, Caroline och Tommy

Innehållsförteckning och kapitel:
====
00:00:00 – Introduktion till avsnittet
00:03:22 – Åtta år senare hos Tommy: bakgrunden och känslomässig närhet
00:06:01 – Tre typer av par: de som bråkar, de som tiger och de frustrerade
00:08:10 – Varför män håller låg profil: könsroller och barndomsmönster
00:11:40 – Omedvetna mönster och den tyste mannen i skuggvärlden
00:18:17 – Lucky Luke som mansbild och Carolines insikt om sårbarhet
00:22:40 – Frustrationen som kärlek: vad den frustrerade egentligen signalerar
00:25:35 – Tillit som grundsten: hur tvivlet blockerar allt i relationen
00:28:00 – Varma ord och bekräftelse blockeras av tvivlet på den andre
00:33:26 – Positivt beroende: anknytningsteorin om att kunna räkna med varandra
00:38:34 – Hur man bryter mönstret: utmana tilliten även när det känns svårt
00:43:45 – Att klaga utan att kräva lösning: behovet av att bli lyssnad på
00:46:21 – Barn och hundar före partner: varför Jan lyssnar annorlunda
00:49:54 – Självkärlek och anknytning: bilden av sig själv och bilden av andra
00:54:55 – Varningstecken: hur man fångar att relationen är på väg utför
00:57:43 – Varför separationer ofta kommer som en chock för en av parterna
00:59:23 – Nyfikenheten på varandra: nyckeln som håller kontakten vid liv
01:01:05 – När par söker hjälp: småbarnsåren, fyrtioårskrisen och tomboet
01:04:28 – Parrelationen som tredje part: Jan, Caroline och relationen var för sig
01:10:43 – Hur man uttrycker behov och undviker obalans i det emotionella ansvaret
01:14:19 – Fördomen att parterapi bara löser logistik, inte kärleken
01:22:13 – Pengabråk och gemensam ekonomi: allt handlar om tillit
01:33:35 – Livsfaser: framgångsrik kvinna, pension och styvbarn i nya relationer
01:38:03 – Icks, röda flaggor och positiv acceptans för en partners skavanker
01:41:33 – När parterapi passar och hur man väljer rätt terapeut
01:49:20 – Gottman och Sue Johnson: forskning om vad som håller ihop par
01:52:04 – Sammanfattning: ta hand om din partner på det sätt som faktiskt tas emot

Länkar från avsnittet:
====
